passages.passage_text: A resident of Delhi for not less than 5 years immediately preceding the date of application. A family income of not more than Rs. 50,000 annually. Not have any dealership in any oil company. Has previous relative as a dealer / distributor of any oil company. Restrictions relating to annual income would not be applicable to persons having own dealership for a period of more than 5 years continuously. In case of government, but the case shall be referred to the Managing Director. An unemployed graduates, conditions (c) and (f) may be waived. If a person belongs to SC/ST but is not a resident of Delhi, the case shall be referred to the Chairman. On the basis of the above conditions and the information provided against each person, decide which of the following courses of action should be taken. Mark answer: 1) if the applicant is to be selected; 2) if the data provided is not to be selected; 3) if the case is inadequate; 4) if the case is to be referred to the Managing Director; and 5) if the case is to be referred to the Chairman. Below is an unemployed graduate who has been living in Delhi since August 1981. He is a citizen of India and his date of birth 2nd February, 1970. His family income is the only source and they are drawing Rs 800 per month.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Sudhir Kashyap was born on 1st July, 1969. He is sponsored by Voltech India Ltd. He possesses a passport, a visa and an eligibility certificate. He is prepared to make a statutory deposit of Rs 25,000. He cannot be sponsored by a Company having an equity capital of Rs 10 crore. He is not required statutory deposit.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Arun Kulshreshtha possesses an eligibility certificate. He has a passport and a visa. She is willing to make a statutory deposit of Rs 25,000. She is not required statutory deposit.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Rakesh Yadav is a B. Tech. and possesses a character certificate and a health certificate. He is sponsored by O. N. P. India Ltd having an equity base of 12.5 crore rupees. He has a TOEFL score of 570. He possesses a passport and a visa. He is 26 years old.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Pranab Chattopadhyay has born on 2nd May 1974. He is ready to make a statutory deposit of Rs 25,000. He has a passport but not visa. He does not have eligibility certificate and is 24 years old.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Shammu Mukherjee is 27 years old. He cannot deposit $ 25,000 as his own, nor is he sponsored by any company. He has a TOEFL score of 600. He possesses a passport, visa and an eligibility certificate.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Arun Kumar Bhadra Singh is willing to make the required deposit. His TOEFL score is 600. He was born on 2nd August, 1973. He possesses a passport but not a visa. Similarly, he has the required health certificate but not the character certificate.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Umesh Chattopadhyay was born on 2nd September, 1974. He has a TOEFL score of 600. He is not capable of making the required deposit of Rs 25,000 sponsored by Company having an equity capital of Rs 10 crore. He has all the relevant certificates as required by the Law College.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Rakesh Mohapatra is a 27-year-old student sponsored by a company having an equity base of Rs 12 crore. He possesses all the required certificates and required. His TOEFL score is 546.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Madan Vaishnaya was born on 2nd September, 1963. He is willing to make the required statutory deposit. He possesses a passport and a visa but yet to receive any health certificate although he has a character certificate. He has a TOEFL score of 600.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Ranjan Mathiuddin is 28 years old. He is willing to make a statutory deposit of upto Rs 9 lakh. He possesses a passport and a visa. He also has the required character certificate. His TOEFL score is 620.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Raja Narayan is a matriculate who has been living in Delhi since August 1985. He was born on 7th November, 1955. His family income is less than Rs. 25,000 per annum and he has no close relatives as a dealer of any oil company.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Kalam Gopal born on 22nd January, 1963 is an Indian by nationality. He is a matriculate having dealership in Tamsha Oil Company. His family income is Rs 21,000 per annum and he is a resident of Delhi since 1974. He has no close relatives as dealer/distributor in any oil company.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Balvinder Singh working in the State Corporation is an Indian by nationality. He is a graduate and his family income is Rs. 60,000 per annum. He has been in Delhi for 7 years. He does not work himself or any of his relatives working as distributor or dealer in any oil company.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Parvesh Kaur, an Indian, born in 1970, is an intermediate staying in Delhi since 1979. He does not hold any dealership in any oil company and the income of his mother, the sole earner, is not more than Rs 500 per month.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Chabitam, an Indian resident of Bombay, is a matriculate with a family income of Rs 20,000 per annum. His date of birth is 15.3.1972. He does not have any dealership in any oil company nor has any close relative as dealer or distributor. He is an SC candidate.
